headlines.title: ‘A war zone’: Venezuela aid workers fear health crisis after earthquakes
headlines.snippet: Aid workers in Venezuela express concerns about a potential health crisis following earthquakes, citing overcrowding and lack of clean water in temporary shelters.

headlines.title: Iran war live: ‘Positive progress’ as US, Tehran wrap up indirect talks
headlines.snippet: Iran and the United States have concluded indirect talks, with Iran stating that a communication channel will be established to discuss potential breaches of a memorandum of understanding.

headlines.title: Couple arrested after daring Empire State marriage proposal stunt
headlines.snippet: Russian daredevils, known for 'rooftopping,' were arrested after performing a marriage proposal stunt at the Empire State Building. They are considered ambassadors for the climbing sport.

headlines.title: US judge sides with NAACP over proposed mail-in ballot restrictions
headlines.snippet: A U.S. judge has ruled in favor of the NAACP regarding proposed restrictions on mail-in ballots. President Trump had sought to limit this voting method.

headlines.title: E Jean Carroll demands Donald Trump pay $5.8m in damages from 2019 case
headlines.snippet: E. Jean Carroll's legal team is seeking an expedited resolution for Donald Trump to pay $5.8 million in damages from a 2019 case, following the U.S. Supreme Court's decision not to hear an appeal.

headlines.title: IMF lowers Israel growth forecast over regional tensions
headlines.snippet: The International Monetary Fund has lowered Israel's 2026 growth forecast to 3.5 percent due to regional tensions. They also anticipate a temporary rise in inflation due to energy prices and supply constraints.

headlines.title: Lebanese foreign minister defends framework agreement with Israel
headlines.snippet: Lebanon's foreign minister is defending a recently signed framework agreement with Israel, stating it is the only path to ensure Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon and secure lasting stability.

headlines.title: Qatari official says 'positive progress' made on US-Iran MoU
headlines.snippet: A Qatari official reported positive progress in discussions related to the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. Further talks are scheduled following funeral processions for the former Iranian Supreme Leader.

headlines.title: Airlines restore some Middle East flights as suspensions continue
headlines.snippet: Some airlines are resuming flights to the Middle East as diplomatic efforts continue, though many suspensions remain in place. Specific routes and end dates for cancellations vary by carrier.

headlines.title: Hormuz shipping may take months to return to normal, French shipping company says
headlines.snippet: A French shipping company expects that normalizing shipping in the Strait of Hormuz could take several months, despite hopes for improvements in the coming days. Several vessels have been stuck in the Gulf.
